promotionCriteria.meaning: The submission lane is relied on as a durable external filing boundary rather than as a local XML emission adapter.
The lane publishes or depends on a stable partner schema, endpoint, namespace, or service-description contract that matters operationally.
Partner acceptance, rejection, receipt, or filing-id semantics must be governed at the lane itself.
Duplicate, retry, replay, or correlation-id behavior must be governed beyond ordinary publication metadata.
Correction, amendment, supersession, or successor-filing behavior must be governed at the filing lane itself.
Compatibility promises for the filing lane must be governed independently of the underlying SubmissionAssembly content.
Release bundles, deployment evidence, or service-description catalogs must be able to name the filing lane directly.

laneChoiceRules.when: A promoted filing lane needs only stable surface identity, binding, version, auth, delivery, and idempotency semantics.
laneChoiceRules.rationale: Ordinary boundary semantics should stay inside the governed runtime-surface family rather than creating a new family by convenience.

laneChoiceRules.when: A promoted filing lane also requires governed receipt, rejection, filing-id, duplicate-resolution, amendment, or successor-filing semantics.
laneChoiceRules.rationale: Once filing-specific semantics materially exceed the generic runtime-surface family, OFARM may justify an equivalent filing-boundary contract lane.

mustRemainOutOfGovernedReleaseLaneUntil: A real partner filing boundary is named directly in release/service-description artifacts.
Partner-controlled receipt and correction semantics are no longer implicit in local adapter code.
The package can decide whether generic RuntimeSurfaceContract is sufficient or an equivalent filing-boundary contract is justified.
currentDecision.rationale: The current package proves governed filing preparation and filing refusal paths, but it still models the partner XML emission as an implementation-local adapter rather than a stable governed filing boundary.

notes: This decision set keeps the current submission XML path explicit while making the future filing-boundary promotion threshold and lane-choice rules machine-readable in implementation support.
